# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_query

  private

    def set_query
      @query = params[:query] || ''
    end

    def execute_query
      begin
        ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(@query)
        flash[:notice] = 'Query executed successfully.'
      rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
        flash[:alert] = 'Error executing the query: ' + e.message
      end
    end

    def execute_and_render_results
      results = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(@query)

      respond_to do |format|
        format.html { render :results, locals: {results: results}}
        format.csv { send_data CSV.generate(results) }
        format.json { render json: results}
      end
    end

    def index
      execute_query
      execute_and_render_results if ActiveRecord::Base.connection.executed_query.success?
    end
end
